BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.
Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. Individual natural wet avalanches are possible. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m. Avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released, even by small loads in isolated cases, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little. Above approximately 1800 m snow will fall in some regions.
The snowpack is moist, in particular on sunny slopes in all altitude zones, as well as on west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. Steep west, north and east facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. Some snow will fall in some regions.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
As a consequence of wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.
From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2900 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. On steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some rain will fall in the afternoon in some regions. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2900 m. The high humditiy will give rise in the afternoon to a loss of strength within the snowpack also on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wet snow requires caution. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Some rain will fall in some regions.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in the afternoon.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible in the afternoon.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Monday: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust.

The weather conditions will give rise to slight moistening of the snowpack.

As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the avalanche danger. More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Monday: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust.

The weather conditions will give rise to slight moistening of the snowpack.

As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the avalanche danger. More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In some regions up to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2700 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The avalanche prone locations for moist avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2300 m. Bases of rock walls are especially unfavourable. From starting zones that have retained some snow on very steep shady slopes moist avalanches can in very isolated cases reach large size. The more recent wind slabs are to be found especially in the vicinity of peaks. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous.
Hardly any increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of the precipitation.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in some places in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2700 m. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack below approximately 2700 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust.
From late morning the natural activity of avalanches will increase.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

Since Sunday mostly small wind slabs formed especially above approximately 3000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The wind will be moderate in some localities.
The weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 2000 m hardly any snow is lying.
The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: More moist and wet avalanches are possible.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase quickly during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust.
From late morning the natural activity of avalanches will increase.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep slopes more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.

Since Sunday mostly small wind slabs formed especially above approximately 3000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The wind will be moderate in some localities.
The weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 2000 m hardly any snow is lying.
The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: More moist and wet avalanches are possible.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place gliding avalanches can reach intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In all aspects more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place. Great caution and restraint are recommended.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures and rain up to high altitudes an unfavourable avalanche situation developed during the last two days. The snowpack is wet. Gliding avalanches can be released at any time of day or night.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Over a wide area rain. As the moisture increases the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Snow cover and thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude, and are steadily decreasing. Weather conditions will further soak the snowpack in many areas. Over a wide area a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be cloudy. Over a wide area rain below approximately 2000 m.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning.

Below approximately 2500 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

In particular on very steep slopes and in steep rocky terrain more small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will already soften in the late morning.

Below approximately 2500 m: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

In particular on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack is not frozen.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation a crust formed on the surface during the last few days.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

In particular on very steep slopes more small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The surface of the snowpack is not frozen.

Below approximately 2200 m no snow is lying on south facing slopes.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation a crust formed on the surface during the last few days.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be partly cloudy over a wide area.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
